rxn_class_summary: Ni-catalyzed enantioselective electroreductive cross-coupling (eRCC) of acrylates with aryl halides and alkyl bromides to give alpha-aryl carbonyls in an undivided cell with sacrificial Fe anode
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction (Ni foam) regenerates low-valent Ni species (Ni(II)->Ni(I) and Ar-Ni(III)->Ar-Ni(II)); Fe sacrificial anode is oxidized to Fe2+ to balance charge

key_intermediates.name: Ar-Ni(III) species (oxidative addition adduct of ArCl to Ni(I))
alkyl radical (tert-butyl radical from t-BuBr via SET with Ni(I))
alpha-ester C-radical intermediate C (alkyl radical addition to acrylate)
Dimeric Ni/BiIM species Ni-1 (two BiIM ligands per Ni, characterized by XRD)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II) precatalyst to Ni(I) (A), which undergoes oxidative addition with ArCl to give Ar-Ni(III); cathodic reduction yields Ar-Ni(II) (B). In parallel, Ni(I) activates alkyl bromide by SET to release an alkyl radical that adds to the acrylate giving radical C, which is captured by B to form Ar-Ni(III)-alkyl D; reductive elimination delivers the product and Ni(I) (E), while Fe anode is oxidized to Fe2+.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 4a not formed (0% yield), consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Mn
probe_reagents.observation: No product without electric current; underscores requirement of electrochemistry

probe_reagents.name: TDAE (tetrakis(dimethylamino)ethylene)
probe_reagents.observation: No product as chemical reductant alone

probe_reagents.name: tert-butyl bromide (3a) added to NiBr2.glyme/L1 in CV
probe_reagents.observation: Reduction peak of Ni(II)/Ni(I) considerably increased, indicating SET from Ni(I) to alkyl bromide generates alkyl radicals

probe_reagents.name: aryl chloride 1a added in CV
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at -0.59 V disappears with increasing 1a; consistent with oxidative addition of ArCl to Ni(I)

probe_reagents.name: Controlled potential electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: No product at -1.0 V; 68% yield/94% ee at -1.8 V; 27% at -2.2 V, supporting Ni(I) (not Ni(0)) as active species

probe_reagents.name: Electrolysis without catalyst
probe_reagents.observation: No radical addition product detected (Fig 1G), excluding direct cathodic generation of alkyl radicals

probe_reagents.name: Nonlinear effect study with chiral L1
probe_reagents.observation: Positive nonlinear effect (ee_prod > ee_L1), indicating a dimeric Ni species with two BiIM ligands is involved in the enantio-determining step

notes: Authors favor Ni(I)/Ni(III) cycle (rather than Ni(0)/Ni(II)) based on CV showing oxidation peak disappearance upon ArCl addition and constant-potential electrolysis at -1.87 V (matching Ni(II)/Ni(I) couple). Positive nonlinear effect suggests a dimeric Ni-BiIM2 species participates in the stereo-determining step; a dimeric Ni species (Ni-1) was crystallographically characterized. Fe anode is sacrificial (oxidized to Fe2+). Chemical reductants (Mn, Zn, TDAE) fail under otherwise identical conditions, highlighting unique role of cathodic reduction.
